Predict from a fitted meep object

Source: R/meep.R
predict.meep.Rd

Out-of-fold predictions exist only for the training rows (and are stored on the fitted object). For genuinely new data, predict.meep() refits each base learner on the full training sample – using the frozen hyperparameters when tune = "once" – then combines the refits with the stored ensemble weights. Full-data refits are cached lazily in an environment field on the object and are not serialized.

Usage

# S3 method for class 'meep'
predict(
  object,
  newdata,
  nuisance = c("outcome", "treatment", "mu0", "mu1"),
  ...
)

Arguments

object

A fitted "meep" object.

newdata

A matrix or data.frame of new covariates.

nuisance

Which nuisance to predict: "outcome", "treatment", "mu0", or "mu1".

...

Unused.

Value

A numeric vector of length nrow(newdata).

Details

Predictions on new data can extrapolate; the cross-fitting honesty guarantee applies only to the training-row OOF predictions.

On this page
